title: lantern/ — R Package
text: R package ancestry-specific rare variant association analysis C backend. v0.1.0, License MIT.

title: lantern/ — R Package
code: lantern/ ├── DESCRIPTION / NAMESPACE        # package metadata ├── R/ │   ├── lantern.R                  # C wrappers + exported R API │   └── workflow.R                 # run_ancestry_pipeline() (overlap handling, monomorphic filter) ├── src/                           # C backend — see lantern/src/AGENTS.md │   ├── ancestry.c                 # split_by_ancestry, count_ancestry_codes │   ├── ancestry.h                 # C function declarations │   ├── init.c                     # R_CallMethodDef registration │   └── Makevars                   # PKG_CFLAGS = -O3 -march=native -Wall └── tests/testthat/     ├── test-functions.R           # count_ancestry_codes + split_by_ancestry     └── test-workflow.R            # run_ancestry_pipeline (overlap, monomorphic)

title: lantern/ — R Package
text: Always go pixi (system gcc 9.3 breaks R 4.5 C23 enum syntax): install fails Rboolean errors, ~/.R/Makevars used instead R.Makevars.local. Confirm R_MAKEVARS_USER=$PWD/R.Makevars.local set task.
code: pixi run install-lantern          # R CMD INSTALL lantern/ pixi run test                     # devtools::test("lantern")

title: lantern/ — R Package
text: C entry registration: every C function listed init.c MUST also matching declaration ancestry.h non-static definition ancestry.c. Mismatch causes linker/compiler errors. R exports: add @export roxygen tag R/lantern.R R/workflow.R, re-run roxygen. Don’t hand-edit NAMESPACE (says “Generated roxygen2: edit hand”). Matrix types: genotype PT matrices must integer storage. R wrappers coerce via storage.mode() <- "integer". Ancestry codes: integer 1/2/3, strings, factors.

title: lantern/ — R Package
text: 5 pre-existing test failures tests/testthat/. Examples: test-functions.R:40 expects african=2 het (1); test-workflow.R monomorphic filter unnamed-matrix code paths logic bugs. env-related — needs code fixes. Build artifacts committed: src/ancestry.o, src/init.o, src/lantern.checked . Add .gitignore next commit. .write_vcf_with_ancestry() call R/workflow.R broken (passes 6 args 5-arg C entry). Currently dead code path — guarded vcf_path + out_prefix non-NULL. Don’t enable without fixing.

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: splitting genotype dosages local ancestry (see vignette("vcf-splitting")), LANTERN feeds resulting ancestry-specific dosage matrices SMMAT (Sequence Kernel Association Mixed Model Association Test) via GMMAT package. Three parallel tests run gene: AA EE p-values can combined Cauchy Fisher meta-analysis step produce single multi-ancestry gene p-value.

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: simulate small dataset mirrors real-data workflow: 60 independent samples, 1 gene 18 rare variants.
code: set.seed(2025) N      <- 60     # samples N_VAR  <- 18     # variants in the toy gene GENE   <- "TOYGENE1" CHROM  <- "1"  # Simulate independent samples (identity kinship) — # in real data replace with the LANTERN_kinship_rds.rds matrix Kmat    <- diag(N) samp_id <- paste0("S", sprintf("%03d", seq_len(N))) rownames(Kmat) <- colnames(Kmat) <- samp_id  # Binary phenotype (case/control) with case fraction ~30% pheno <- rbinom(N, 1, 0.3) df_pheno <- data.frame(id = samp_id, y = pheno)

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: start raw unphased GT matrix per-variant ancestry code matrix, call LANTERN C backend.
code:
# Rare variant dosages: mostly 0, occasionally 1, rarely 2 gt_raw <- matrix(   sample(0:2, N_VAR * N, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.90, 0.09, 0.01)),   nrow = N_VAR, ncol = N ) rownames(gt_raw) <- paste0(CHROM, ":", seq(1e6, by = 500, length.out = N_VAR)) colnames(gt_raw) <- samp_id  # Ancestry codes: 1=EUR, 2=MIX, 3=AFR — admixed cohort with ~40% AFR anc_raw <- matrix(   sample(1:3, N_VAR * N, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.35, 0.25, 0.40)),   nrow = N_VAR, ncol = N,   dimnames = dimnames(gt_raw) )  split_out <- split_by_ancestry(gt_raw, anc_raw) ds_afr <- split_out$african    # variants × samples, African ancestry dosage ds_eur <- split_out$european   # variants × samples, European ancestry dosage  cat("AFR dosage: ", nrow(ds_afr), "variants ×", ncol(ds_afr), "samples
") #> AFR dosage:  18 variants × 60 samples cat("EUR dosage: ", nrow(ds_eur), "variants ×", ncol(ds_eur), "samples
") #> EUR dosage:  18 variants × 60 samples cat("AFR mean per-variant dosage:", round(mean(ds_afr), 4), "
") #> AFR mean per-variant dosage: 0.0513 cat("EUR mean per-variant dosage:", round(mean(ds_eur), 4), "
") #> EUR mean per-variant dosage: 0.064

what: 2 Packaging dosage matrices into GDS files
title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: SMMAT reads variants SeqArray GDS files DS (dosage) FORMAT field. create temporary VCFs.
code:
# Helper: write a minimal VCF with GT:DS format write_ds_vcf <- function(path, dosage_mat, sample_ids,                          chrom, positions, ref, alt) {   n_var  <- nrow(dosage_mat)   n_samp <- ncol(dosage_mat)   gt_map <- c("0/0", "0/1", "1/1")    header <- c(     "##fileformat=VCFv4.1",     '##FORMAT=<ID=GT,Number=1,Type=String,Description="Genotype">',     paste0('##FORMAT=<ID=DS,Number=1,Type=Float,',            'Description="Ancestry-specific dosage of the alternate allele">'),     paste(c("#CHROM", "POS", "ID", "REF", "ALT",             "QUAL", "FILTER", "INFO", "FORMAT", sample_ids),           collapse = "	")   )    data_lines <- vapply(seq_len(n_var), function(i) {     ds        <- dosage_mat[i, ]     gt_idx    <- pmin(2L, pmax(0L, as.integer(round(ds))))     gt_str    <- gt_map[gt_idx + 1L]     samp_flds <- paste0(gt_str, ":", sprintf("%.4f", ds))     paste(c(chrom, sprintf("%d", as.integer(positions[i])), ".", ref[i], alt[i],             ".", "PASS", ".", "GT:DS", samp_flds),           collapse = "	")   }, character(1L))    writeLines(c(header, data_lines), path) } library(SeqArray)  # Variant metadata matching our simulated positions positions <- seq(1000000L, by = 500L, length.out = N_VAR) ref_allele <- rep("A", N_VAR) alt_allele <- rep("T", N_VAR)  # Create temporary VCF → GDS for each ancestry vcf_afr <- tempfile(fileext = ".vcf") vcf_eur <- tempfile(fileext = ".vcf") gds_afr <- tempfile(fileext = ".gds") gds_eur <- tempfile(fileext = ".gds")  write_ds_vcf(vcf_afr, ds_afr, samp_id, CHROM, positions, ref_allele, alt_allele) write_ds_vcf(vcf_eur, ds_eur, samp_id, CHROM, positions, ref_allele, alt_allele)  seqVCF2GDS(vcf_afr, gds_afr, verbose = FALSE) seqVCF2GDS(vcf_eur, gds_eur, verbose = FALSE)  cat("GDS files created:
", gds_afr, "
", gds_eur, "
") #> GDS files created: #>  /tmp/RtmpMRxaUi/file1dde1760104e.gds  #>  /tmp/RtmpMRxaUi/file1dde60723af.gds

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: SMMAT looks variants per gene tab-delimited file header: weight column typically 1 (unweighted burden) function MAF (e.g. Beta(1,25) weights).
code: gene_name   chrom   pos   ref   alt   weight gene_group_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".tsv") gene_df <- data.frame(   gene  = GENE,   chr   = CHROM,   pos   = positions,   ref   = ref_allele,   alt   = alt_allele,   weight = 1 ) write.table(gene_df, gene_group_file,             sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, col.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)  cat("Gene-group file (first 3 rows):\n") #> Gene-group file (first 3 rows): head(read.table(gene_group_file, sep = "\t"), 3) #>         V1 V2      V3 V4   V5 V6 #> 1 TOYGENE1  1 1000000  A TRUE  1 #> 2 TOYGENE1  1 1000500  A TRUE  1 #> 3 TOYGENE1  1 1001000  A TRUE  1

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: null mixed model fit reused three SMMAT calls: Note: GMMAT >= 1.4 required. real analyses kinship matrix (kins) sparse relatedness matrix estimated genotype data; pass identity matrix assume independence.
code: library(GMMAT)  # glmmkin: logistic GLMM for case/control phenotype # For a continuous trait use family = gaussian(link = "identity") model0 <- glmmkin(   y ~ 1,   data   = df_pheno,   kins   = Kmat,   id     = "id",   family = binomial(link = "logit") )

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: result data.frame one row per gene columns: gene, N.variant, N.sample, B, B.var, Q_B, p_B, Q_S, p_S, p_SMMAT (primary omnibus p-value).
code: run_smmat <- function(gds_file, is_dosage) {   SMMAT(     model0,     gds.fn      = gds_file,     group.file  = gene_group_file,     MAF.range   = c(0, 0.05),  # rare variants only (MAF < 5%)     miss.cutoff = 1,            # keep all variants     method      = "davies",     # numerical integration for p-values     is.dosage   = is_dosage,    # TRUE for ancestry-specific dosage     ncores      = 1L   ) }  res_aa  <- run_smmat(gds_afr, is_dosage = TRUE) res_ee  <- run_smmat(gds_eur, is_dosage = TRUE) res_obs <- run_smmat(gds_obs, is_dosage = FALSE)  # raw unphased GT

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: significant p_cauchy p_aa /p_ee extreme p_obs suggests signal ancestry-specific — key LANTERN finding.
code: # Bind AA / EE / observed into a single data.frame for the gene combined <- data.frame(   gene     = res_aa$gene,   p_aa     = res_aa$p_SMMAT,   p_ee     = res_ee$p_SMMAT,   p_obs    = res_obs$p_SMMAT )  # Cauchy combination of AA and EE p-values into a multi-ancestry p-value cauchy_p <- function(p1, p2) {   # Han & Liu (2020) Cauchy combination test   T_stat <- 0.5 * (tan((0.5 - p1) * pi) + tan((0.5 - p2) * pi))   pcauchy(T_stat, lower.tail = FALSE) }  combined$p_cauchy <- mapply(cauchy_p, combined$p_aa, combined$p_ee)  print(combined)

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
code: unlink(c(vcf_afr, vcf_eur, gds_afr, gds_eur, gene_group_file))

title: Ancestry-stratified gene-level burden tests
text: See vignette("vcf-splitting") first step workflow.
code: Phased VCF + RFMix MSP          │          ▼   run_phased_pipeline()          │     ┌────┴────┐     │         │ african_ds.gds  european_ds.gds     │         │     └────┬────┘          │     glmmkin()  ← phenotype + kinship          │     ┌────┼────┐     │    │    │  SMMAT SMMAT SMMAT   AA    EE   obs     │    │   Cauchy combination          │   gene-level p-value

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: standard LANTERN workflow splits variant dosages two ancestry-specific pools (African European). study cohort three ancestral backgrounds — example Latino cohort African, European, Native American contributions — two-population assumption restrictive. LANTERN provides two K-population splitting functions: return named list K matrices (variants × samples), one per population. 2-population special case numerically identical split_by_ancestry() split_phased() respectively.

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: diploid ancestry code K populations encodes combination two parental backgrounds present. supply two objects: pure_codes — named integer vector, one entry per population: mixed_codes — data frame columns code, pop1, pop2. One row per mixed-ancestry diploid type: K populations K(K−1)/2 mixed-pair types:
code: c(AFR = 3L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 4L) data.frame(   code = c(2L, 5L, 6L),   pop1 = c("AFR", "AFR", "EUR"),   pop2 = c("EUR", "NAT", "NAT") )

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
code:
set.seed(2025) N_VAR  <- 12   # variants N_SAMP <- 30   # samples  # Ancestry code system #   Pure:  AFR=3, EUR=1, NAT=4 #   Mixed: AFR/EUR=2, AFR/NAT=5, EUR/NAT=6 pure_codes <- c(AFR = 3L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 4L) mixed_codes <- data.frame(   code = c(2L,    5L,    6L   ),   pop1 = c("AFR", "AFR", "EUR"),   pop2 = c("EUR", "NAT", "NAT") )  # Simulate rare-variant genotype matrix (mostly 0, rare 1/2) gt <- matrix(   sample(0:2, N_VAR * N_SAMP, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.88, 0.10, 0.02)),   nrow = N_VAR, ncol = N_SAMP ) rownames(gt) <- paste0("chr19:", seq(1e6, by = 500L, length.out = N_VAR)) colnames(gt) <- paste0("S", sprintf("%02d", seq_len(N_SAMP)))  # Simulate ancestry codes reflecting a tri-admixed cohort #   Roughly: 30% AFR, 20% EUR, 10% NAT, 20% AFR/EUR, 10% AFR/NAT, 10% EUR/NAT all_codes <- c(pure_codes, mixed_codes$code) code_probs <- c(0.30, 0.20, 0.10, 0.20, 0.10, 0.10) anc <- matrix(   sample(all_codes, N_VAR * N_SAMP, replace = TRUE, prob = code_probs),   nrow = N_VAR, ncol = N_SAMP ) out <- split_by_ancestry_multi(gt, anc, pure_codes, mixed_codes)  cat("Output list names:", names(out), "
") #> Output list names: AFR EUR NAT cat("Dimensions (each matrix):", nrow(out$AFR), "variants x",     ncol(out$AFR), "samples

") #> Dimensions (each matrix): 12 variants x 30 samples  cat("AFR dosage (first 4 variants, 6 samples):
") #> AFR dosage (first 4 variants, 6 samples): round(out$AFR[1:4, 1:6], 3) #>      [,1] [,2]  [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] #> [1,]    0    0 0.000    0    0    0 #> [2,]    0    0 0.667    0    0    0 #> [3,]    0    0 0.000    1    0    0 #> [4,]    0    0 0.000    0    0    0 cat("EUR dosage (first 4 variants, 6 samples):
") #> EUR dosage (first 4 variants, 6 samples): round(out$EUR[1:4, 1:6], 3) #>      [,1] [,2]  [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] #> [1,]    0    0 0.000    0    0    0 #> [2,]    0    0 0.333    0    0    0 #> [3,]    0    0 0.000    1    0    1 #> [4,]    0    0 0.000    1    0    0  # Conservation check: per-element sum across pools never exceeds raw gt total <- out$AFR + out$EUR + out$NAT excess <- max(total - gt) cat("
Max excess of pooled dosage over raw gt:", excess,     "(must be <= 0)
") #>  #> Max excess of pooled dosage over raw gt: 0 (must be <= 0) cat("Min dosage value across all pools:", min(out$AFR, out$EUR, out$NAT), "
") #> Min dosage value across all pools: 0

what: 1.3 How p1, p2, p3 are estimated
title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: variant, function estimates population allele proportions unambiguous observations (pure-ancestry samples genotype, plus mixed hom-alts contribute one alt allele parent pool). Ambiguous mixed-ancestry heterozygotes excluded estimation split using result. D=∑k(2Nkk(2)+Nkk(1))+2∑<jNij(2)D = \sum_{k} \bigl(2N_{kk}^{(2)} + N_{kk}^{(1)}\bigr) + 2\sum_{<j} N_{ij}^{(2)} pk=2Nkk(2)+Nkk(1)+∑j≠kN⟨kj⟩(2)D,∑kpk=1p_k = \frac{2N_{kk}^{(2)} + N_{kk}^{(1)} + \sum_{j \neq k} N_{\langle kj \rangle}^{(2)}}{D}, \quad \sum_k p_k = 1 mixed-ancestry heterozygote pair (, j) receives: pi/(pi+pj)p_i/(p_i+p_j) dosage pool pj/(pi+pj)p_j/(p_i+p_j) pool j.
code: # Trace p_k for a single variant by hand var_idx <- 3   # pick variant 3  gt_v   <- gt[var_idx, ] anc_v  <- anc[var_idx, ]  # Pure AFR (code 3) N_AA2 <- sum(anc_v == 3 & gt_v == 2) N_AA1 <- sum(anc_v == 3 & gt_v == 1) # Pure EUR (code 1) N_EE2 <- sum(anc_v == 1 & gt_v == 2) N_EE1 <- sum(anc_v == 1 & gt_v == 1) # Pure NAT (code 4) N_NN2 <- sum(anc_v == 4 & gt_v == 2) N_NN1 <- sum(anc_v == 4 & gt_v == 1) # Mixed hom-alts N_AE2 <- sum(anc_v == 2 & gt_v == 2) N_AN2 <- sum(anc_v == 5 & gt_v == 2) N_EN2 <- sum(anc_v == 6 & gt_v == 2)  D  <- 2*N_AA2 + N_AA1 + 2*N_EE2 + N_EE1 + 2*N_NN2 + N_NN1 +       2*N_AE2 + 2*N_AN2 + 2*N_EN2 p1 <- if (D > 0) (2*N_AA2 + N_AA1 + N_AE2 + N_AN2) / D else 0.5 p2 <- if (D > 0) (2*N_EE2 + N_EE1 + N_AE2 + N_EN2) / D else 0.5 p3 <- if (D > 0) (2*N_NN2 + N_NN1 + N_AN2 + N_EN2) / D else 0.5  cat(sprintf("Variant %d  D=%d  p_AFR=%.3f  p_EUR=%.3f  p_NAT=%.3f\n",             var_idx, as.integer(D), p1, p2, p3)) #> Variant 3  D=5  p_AFR=0.600  p_EUR=0.400  p_NAT=0.000 cat("p1+p2+p3 =", p1 + p2 + p3, "\n") #> p1+p2+p3 = 1

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: denominator D = 0 given variant (alt alleles carried exclusively mixed-ancestry heterozygotes, pure-ancestry mixed hom-alt alt alleles), function falls back 50/50 split within affected pair.
code: # One sample, one variant: AFR/EUR mixed het, no other data gt_s  <- matrix(1L, nrow = 1, ncol = 1) anc_s <- matrix(2L, nrow = 1, ncol = 1)   # code 2 = AFR/EUR r_s   <- split_by_ancestry_multi(gt_s, anc_s, pure_codes, mixed_codes) cat("Singleton AFR/EUR het -> AFR:", r_s$AFR[1,1],     " EUR:", r_s$EUR[1,1],     " NAT:", r_s$NAT[1,1], "\n") #> Singleton AFR/EUR het -> AFR: 0.5  EUR: 0.5  NAT: 0

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: per-haplotype ancestry calls available (e.g. RFMix), split deterministic: haplotype’s allele goes directly population pool. probability estimation needed.

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: split_phased_multi() takes single named integer vector pop_codes length K. codes must match MSP file (RFMix default: 0-based integers).
code: # RFMix codes: AFR=0, EUR=1, NAT=2 pop_codes_phased <- c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 2L)

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: phased function exact every haplotype allele goes exactly one pool; probabilistic component.
code:
set.seed(2025) N_VAR2  <- 8 N_SAMP2 <- 6  # Per-haplotype allele matrices (0/1) gt_hap0 <- matrix(   sample(0:1, N_VAR2 * N_SAMP2, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.9, 0.1)),   nrow = N_VAR2, ncol = N_SAMP2 ) gt_hap1 <- matrix(   sample(0:1, N_VAR2 * N_SAMP2, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.9, 0.1)),   nrow = N_VAR2, ncol = N_SAMP2 )  # Per-haplotype ancestry codes (0=AFR, 1=EUR, 2=NAT) anc_hap0 <- matrix(   sample(0:2, N_VAR2 * N_SAMP2, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.4, 0.2)),   nrow = N_VAR2, ncol = N_SAMP2 ) anc_hap1 <- matrix(   sample(0:2, N_VAR2 * N_SAMP2, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.4, 0.2)),   nrow = N_VAR2, ncol = N_SAMP2 )  phased_out <- split_phased_multi(gt_hap0, gt_hap1,                                   anc_hap0, anc_hap1,                                   pop_codes_phased)  cat("Output names:", names(phased_out), "
") #> Output names: AFR EUR NAT cat("AFR dosage (first 4 variants):
") #> AFR dosage (first 4 variants): print(phased_out$AFR[1:4, ]) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] #> [1,]    0    0    0    0    0    0 #> [2,]    0    0    0    1    0    0 #> [3,]    0    0    0    0    0    0 #> [4,]    0    1    0    0    0    0 # Exact reconstruction: AFR + EUR + NAT == hap0 + hap1 everywhere raw_diploid <- gt_hap0 + gt_hap1 recon_error <- max(abs((phased_out$AFR + phased_out$EUR + phased_out$NAT) -                          raw_diploid)) cat("Max reconstruction error:", recon_error, "(must be 0)
") #> Max reconstruction error: 0 (must be 0)

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: split_phased_multi() K = 2 numerically identical split_phased().
code: pop2 <- c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L) gt0b <- matrix(c(1L,0L,1L,0L), 2, 2) gt1b <- matrix(c(0L,1L,0L,1L), 2, 2) a0b  <- matrix(c(0L,1L,0L,1L), 2, 2) a1b  <- matrix(c(1L,0L,1L,0L), 2, 2)  m2 <- split_phased_multi(gt0b, gt1b, a0b, a1b, pop2) p2 <- split_phased(gt0b, gt1b, a0b, a1b, pop2)  cat("AFR identical:", identical(unname(m2$AFR), p2$african), "\n") #> AFR identical: TRUE cat("EUR identical:", identical(unname(m2$EUR), p2$european), "\n") #> EUR identical: TRUE

title: Multi-ancestry splitting (K >= 3 populations)
text: interface extends directly four five populations adding entries pure_codes rows mixed_codes. K(K−1)/2 mixed-pair codes K populations. K = 4 6 codes; K = 5 10. mixed_codes data frame must list . See ?split_by_ancestry_multi ?split_phased_multi full argument documentation.
code: # Four populations: AFR, EUR, NAT, EAS pure_4  <- c(AFR = 3L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 4L, EAS = 5L) mixed_4 <- data.frame(   code = c(2L, 6L, 7L, 8L, 9L, 10L),   pop1 = c("AFR","AFR","AFR","EUR","EUR","NAT"),   pop2 = c("EUR","NAT","EAS","NAT","EAS","EAS") ) # split_by_ancestry_multi(gt, anc, pure_4, mixed_4) → list of 4 matrices  # Five populations: AFR, EUR, NAT, EAS, SAS pure_5  <- c(AFR=3L, EUR=1L, NAT=4L, EAS=5L, SAS=6L) # mixed_5: 10 rows (one per pair)

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: LANTERN splits variant dosages local ancestry downstream association tests can run separately African (AA) European (EE) haplotype backgrounds admixed individuals. Two input modes supported:

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: split_by_ancestry() takes two matrices identical dimensions: gt_genotype — integer matrix, rows = variants, cols = samples. Values: 0 (hom-ref), 1 (het), 2 (hom-alt). ancestry — integer matrix, shape. Ancestry codes: 1 = EUR/EUR, 2 = AFR/EUR (mixed het), 3 = AFR/AFR. ancestry code summarises haplotypes locus (e.g. RFMix MSP file collapsed per-variant diploid code).

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
code:
set.seed(42) n_var  <- 10   # variants n_samp <- 6    # samples  # Simulate integer genotypes (0/1/2) gt <- matrix(   sample(0:2, n_var * n_samp, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1)),   nrow = n_var, ncol = n_samp ) rownames(gt) <- paste0("var", seq_len(n_var)) colnames(gt) <- paste0("S", seq_len(n_samp))  # Simulate ancestry codes (1=EUR, 2=MIX, 3=AFR) anc <- matrix(   sample(1:3, n_var * n_samp, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.2, 0.4)),   nrow = n_var, ncol = n_samp )  result <- split_by_ancestry(gt, anc) cat("African dosage (first 4 variants × 6 samples):
") #> African dosage (first 4 variants × 6 samples): round(result$african[1:4, ], 2) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] #> [1,]    2    0    2 1.00    0    0 #> [2,]    1    0    0 0.00    0    0 #> [3,]    0    0    0 0.00    0    0 #> [4,]    1    0    2 0.67    0    1  cat("
European dosage (first 4 variants × 6 samples):
") #>  #> European dosage (first 4 variants × 6 samples): round(result$european[1:4, ], 2) #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] [,4] [,5] [,6] #> [1,]    0    0    0 0.00    0    0 #> [2,]    1    1    0 1.00    0    0 #> [3,]    0    2    2 0.00    0    0 #> [4,]    0    0    0 0.33    2    0

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: variant function computes ancestry-specific proportions p1 (African) p2 (European) diploid counts across samples: denominator = total_alt_alleles − N_MIX_het (mixed-het samples assigned stochastically via p1/p2 rather excluded denominator). Singleton edge-case: alt alleles carried mixed-het individuals, p1 = p2 = 0.5 avoid dividing zero.
code:
p1 = (2·N_AA_hom + N_AA_het + N_MIX_hom) / denominator p2 = (N_MIX_hom + 2·N_EUR_hom + N_EUR_het) / denominator # A variant where only mixed-het (code 2) samples carry the alt allele gt_edge  <- matrix(c(1L, 0L, 1L), nrow = 1)   # three samples: het, ref, het anc_edge <- matrix(c(2L, 1L, 2L), nrow = 1)   # all alt carriers are MIX  r <- split_by_ancestry(gt_edge, anc_edge) cat("Singleton MIX case — p1 = p2 = 0.5
") #> Singleton MIX case — p1 = p2 = 0.5 cat("African: ", r$african, "
European:", r$european, "
") #> African:  0.5 0 0.5  #> European: 0.5 0 0.5

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: run_ancestry_pipeline() wraps splitting full pipeline handles sample/variant overlap, monomorphic filtering, optional VCF output. inputs : gt_matrix: variant × sample genotype matrix (rownames = variant IDs, colnames = sample IDs). pt_matrix: sample × region ancestry matrix (rownames = sample IDs, colnames match variant IDs genomic region strings).
code:
set.seed(7) n_v <- 15; n_s <- 8  gt_full <- matrix(   sample(0:2, n_v * n_s, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.6, 0.3, 0.1)),   nrow = n_v, ncol = n_s,   dimnames = list(     paste0("chr1:", 1000 * seq_len(n_v)),   # rownames: variant positions     paste0("samp_", seq_len(n_s))            # colnames: sample IDs   ) )  # PT matrix: rows = samples, cols = same variant IDs pt_full <- matrix(   sample(1:3, n_s * n_v, replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.35, 0.3, 0.35)),   nrow = n_s, ncol = n_v,   dimnames = list(     paste0("samp_", seq_len(n_s)),     paste0("chr1:", 1000 * seq_len(n_v))   ) )  out <- run_ancestry_pipeline(gt_full, pt_full, verbose = FALSE)  cat("African matrix: ", nrow(out$african), "variants ×",     ncol(out$african), "samples
") #> African matrix:  15 variants × 8 samples cat("European matrix:", nrow(out$european), "variants ×",     ncol(out$european), "samples
") #> European matrix: 15 variants × 8 samples cat("Variants kept:", out$overlap$n_variants_kept, "of",     out$overlap$n_variants_total, "
") #> Variants kept: 15 of 15

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: RFMix (equivalent) provides per-haplotype local ancestry calls, LANTERN can deterministically assign haplotype’s allele correct ancestry background — probabilistic splitting needed.

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: Inputs four matrices shape (variants × samples): rule simple: cell, hap0 contributes allele AFR count anc_hap0 == 0, EUR anc_hap0 == 1, nothing otherwise. hap1.
code: # 3 variants × 4 samples gt0 <- matrix(c(1L, 0L, 1L,   # variant 1: hap0 alleles for 3 samples                 0L, 1L, 0L,   # variant 2                 1L, 1L, 0L),  # variant 3               nrow = 3, ncol = 3)  gt1 <- matrix(c(0L, 1L, 0L,                 1L, 0L, 1L,                 0L, 1L, 1L),               nrow = 3, ncol = 3)  # Haplotype ancestry: 0 = AFR, 1 = EUR  (RFMix convention) a0 <- matrix(c(0L, 0L, 1L,                1L, 0L, 0L,                0L, 1L, 0L),              nrow = 3, ncol = 3)  a1 <- matrix(c(1L, 1L, 0L,                0L, 1L, 1L,                1L, 0L, 1L),              nrow = 3, ncol = 3)  phased_result <- split_phased(gt0, gt1, a0, a1)  cat("African dosage (phased):\n"); print(phased_result$african) #> African dosage (phased): #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    1    1    1 #> [2,]    0    1    1 #> [3,]    0    0    0 cat("European dosage (phased):\n"); print(phased_result$european) #> European dosage (phased): #>      [,1] [,2] [,3] #> [1,]    0    0    0 #> [2,]    1    0    1 #> [3,]    1    1    1

title: Splitting genotypes by local ancestry
text: run_phased_pipeline() orchestrates complete workflow raw files: Parse RFMix MSP file (ancestry tracts). Query sample IDs VCF via bcftools. Intersect samples VCF MSP. Parse phased GT strings VCF output. Map variant ancestry tract findInterval(). Broadcast tract-level ancestry variant level. Call split_phased() via C backend. Filter monomorphic variants. Optionally write ancestry-specific VCFs DS (dosage) field convert GDS format. output african_ds.gds / european_ds.gds files primary input downstream gene-level tests (see vignette("gene-level-test")).
code: # Requires bcftools in PATH and real phased VCF + MSP files result <- run_phased_pipeline(   vcf_path = "data/chr19.phased.bcf",   msp_path = "data/chr19.msp.tsv.gz",   out_path  = "output/phased/",   chrom     = "chr19",   write_vcf = TRUE,   verbose   = TRUE )  # result$african  — dosage matrix, rows=variants, cols=samples # result$european — dosage matrix # result$variant_info — data.frame: chrom, pos, ref, alt # result$vcf_paths    — paths to african_ds.vcf, european_ds.vcf, *.gds

title: LANTERN
text: Local Ancestry-Noted Transmission Region Nucleotides R package Ancestry-Specific Rare Variant Association Analysis pure C backend.

title: LANTERN
text: Pure C backend performance-critical operations Efficient matrix operations ancestry code counting Genotype splitting ancestry (African/European) Direct data frame/matrix input (PLINK dependency) Automatic overlap handling sample variant mismatches Monomorphic filtering - removes variants alt alleles

title: LANTERN
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("yuw444/LANTERN", subdir = "lantern")  # Or from local source R CMD INSTALL lantern/

title: LANTERN
code: library(lantern)  # GT matrix: 5 variants x 4 samples # 0 = homozygous ref, 1 = heterozygous, 2 = homozygous alt gt <- matrix(c(2, 1, 0, 1, 2, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0,                1, 1, 1, 0, 2, 1, 0, 1, 0, 1),               nrow = 5, ncol = 4)  # PT matrix: parent-of-origin ancestry codes (rows=samples, cols=variants) # 1 = EUR/EUR, 2 = AFR/EUR (mixed), 3 = AFR/AFR pt <- matrix(c(3, 2, 1, 3, 2, 1, 2, 2, 1, 1,                3, 1, 2, 1, 3, 2, 1, 2, 1, 3),               nrow = 4, ncol = 5)  # Run full pipeline result <- run_ancestry_pipeline(gt, pt) result$african    # African ancestry-specific dosages result$european   # European ancestry-specific dosages result$counts     # Ancestry counts per region

title: LANTERN
text: Rows: Samples (must sample_id column rownames) Columns: Genomic regions/windows 1 = EUR/EUR (Pure European) 2 = AFR/EUR (Mixed) 3 = AFR/AFR (Pure African)

title: LANTERN
text: Rows: Variants Columns: Samples (must match PT matrix columns) Values: 0, 1, 2 (dosage alternate allele)

title: LANTERN
text: pipeline automatically handles mismatches GT PT matrices:

title: LANTERN
code: # GT has samples A, B, C # PT has samples A, B, D # -> Only A and B are used  gt <- matrix(0, nrow = 2, ncol = 3,              dimnames = list(c("v1", "v2"), c("A", "B", "C"))) pt <- matrix(1, nrow = 3, ncol = 2,              dimnames = list(c("A", "B", "D"), c("v1", "v2")))  result <- run_ancestry_pipeline(gt, pt) # result$overlap$n_samples_kept = 2 # result$overlap$dropped_samples = c("C", "D")

title: LANTERN
code: # GT variants: chr22:100, chr22:200, chr22:300 # PT regions: chr22:50-150, chr22:150-250 # -> Only chr22:100 and chr22:200 are used (matched by coordinate)  gt <- matrix(0, nrow = 3, ncol = 2,              dimnames = list(c("chr22:100", "chr22:200", "chr22:300"),                              c("s1", "s2"))) pt <- matrix(1, nrow = 2, ncol = 2,              dimnames = list(c("s1", "s2"),                              c("chr22:50-150", "chr22:150-250")))  result <- run_ancestry_pipeline(gt, pt) # result$overlap$n_variants_kept = 2

title: LANTERN
text: heterozygous genotypes (gt=1) mixed ancestry (pt=2), algorithm uses population-based proportions (p1, p2):

title: LANTERN
text: N1-N8 counts per variant:

title: LANTERN
text: Singleton: alt alleles come mixed het individuals (N5 = sum(gt)), p1 = p2 = 0.5 Homozygous alt (gt=2): 1 allele ancestry regardless pt Pure ancestry (pt=1 pt=3): alt alleles ancestry

title: LANTERN

title: Parse RFMix MSP file — .parse_msp
text: Parse RFMix MSP (local ancestry tract) file ancestry matrices. MSP format: two header lines followed data rows. Line 1: population codes (e.g., "#Subpopulation order/codes: AFR=0  EUR=1"). Line 2: column headers. Data rows: ancestry calls per haplotype per tract.

title: lantern: High-Level Workflow Functions — run_ancestry_pipeline
text: Convenience functions common ancestry-specific analysis workflows. Run ancestry splitting pipeline

title: lantern: High-Level Workflow Functions — run_ancestry_pipeline
code: run_ancestry_pipeline(gt_matrix, pt_matrix, verbose = TRUE)

title: lantern: High-Level Workflow Functions — run_ancestry_pipeline
text: gt_matrix Integer matrix genotypes (rows=variants, cols=samples) Values: 0=homozygous ref, 1=heterozygous, 2=homozygous alt rownames: variant IDs (e.g., "chr:pos" identifier) colnames: sample IDs pt_matrix Integer matrix parent--origin ancestry codes (rows=samples, cols=regions) Values: 1=EUR/EUR, 2=AFR/EUR (mixed), 3=AFR/AFR rownames: sample IDs (must match colnames gt_matrix) colnames: region IDs (e.g., "chr:start-end") verbose Print progress messages (default TRUE)

title: lantern: High-Level Workflow Functions — run_ancestry_pipeline
text: List elements: african African ancestry-specific dosage matrix european European ancestry-specific dosage matrix counts List ancestry counts per region overlap Overlap information

title: lantern: High-Level Workflow Functions — run_ancestry_pipeline
text: Complete pipeline: split genotype matrix ancestry, return dosage matrices. Handles sample variant mismatches using overlapping data. Memory efficient: single-pass subsetting using index vectors.

title: lantern: High-Level Workflow Functions — run_ancestry_pipeline
code: # Example with mismatched dimensions gt <- matrix(c(2, 1, 0, 1, 2, 1), nrow = 2, ncol = 3,              dimnames = list(c("chr1:100", "chr1:200"),                             c("sample_A", "sample_B", "sample_C")))  pt <- matrix(c(3, 2, 1, 3, 2, 1), nrow = 3, ncol = 2,              dimnames = list(c("sample_A", "sample_B", "sample_D"),                             c("chr1:50-150", "chr1:150-250")))  result <- run_ancestry_pipeline(gt, pt) #> === LANTERN Ancestry Pipeline === #> Step 1: Finding sample overlap... #>   Samples: 3 in GT, 3 in PT #>   Common samples: 2 #>   Dropped: sample_C, sample_D #>  #> Step 2: Finding variant/region overlap... #>   No exact matches. Trying coordinate-based matching... #>   Found 2 variants in 2 regions #>   Variants in GT: 2 #>   Regions in PT: 2 #>   Common variants/regions: 4 #>  #> Step 3: Subsetting matrices... #>  #> Step 3b: Filtering monomorphic variants... #>  #> Step 4: Splitting genotypes by ancestry... #>   -> African dosage matrix: 2 x 2 #>   -> European dosage matrix: 2 x 2 #>  #> Step 5: Counting ancestries per region... #>   -> African (3): median = 1 #>   -> European (1): median = 0 #>   -> Mixed (2): median = 1 #>  #> === Pipeline Complete === # Only sample_A and sample_B are kept (common to both) # Only chr1:100 and chr1:200 that overlap regions are kept

what: Run phased ancestry splitting pipeline — run_phased_pipeline
title: Run phased ancestry splitting pipeline — run_phased_pipeline
text: Complete pipeline phased data: parse RFMix MSP file phased VCF, intersect samples, map variant ancestry tract, call split_phased via C backend, optionally write ancestry-specific VCFs DS field can converted GDS.

title: Run phased ancestry splitting pipeline — run_phased_pipeline
code: run_phased_pipeline(   vcf_path,   msp_path,   out_path,   chrom = NULL,   write_vcf = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run phased ancestry splitting pipeline — run_phased_pipeline
text: vcf_path Path phased VCF/BCF file (plain gzipped). bcftools must PATH. msp_path Path RFMix MSP file (plain text gzipped TSV). out_path Output directory VCFs, GDS, cache files. chrom Chromosome process (e.g., "chr19" "19"). NULL, chromosomes present VCF used. write_vcf Logical; TRUE, write ancestry-specific VCFs convert GDS format using SeqArray::seqVCF2GDS(). verbose Print step--step progress messages.

title: Run phased ancestry splitting pipeline — run_phased_pipeline
text: Invisibly, list elements: african Numeric matrix (variants × samples) African dosages. european Numeric matrix (variants × samples) European dosages. variant_info data.frame columns chrom, pos, ref, alt. sample_ids Character vector common sample IDs. tract_info data.frame ancestry tracts MSP file. overlap List intersection statistics. vcf_paths (write_vcf = TRUE) Named list output     file paths.

title: Run phased ancestry splitting pipeline — run_phased_pipeline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ result <- run_phased_pipeline(   vcf_path = "data/chr19.phased.bcf",   msp_path = "data/chr19.msp.tsv.gz",   out_path = "output/",   chrom    = "chr19",   write_vcf = TRUE ) head(result$variant_info) } # }

what: Split genotype matrix by ancestry — split_by_ancestry
title: Split genotype matrix by ancestry — split_by_ancestry
text: Split genotype matrix African European ancestry-specific dosage matrices based parent--origin ancestry matrix.

title: Split genotype matrix by ancestry — split_by_ancestry
code: split_by_ancestry(gt_genotype, ancestry)

title: Split genotype matrix by ancestry — split_by_ancestry
text: gt_genotype Integer matrix genotypes (0, 1, 2) - Rows represent variants - Columns represent samples ancestry Integer matrix ancestry codes (1, 2, 3) - dimensions gt_genotype - Values: 1=EUR/EUR, 2=AFR/EUR (mixed), 3=AFR/AFR

title: Split genotype matrix by ancestry — split_by_ancestry
text: List two elements: african Matrix African ancestry-specific dosages european Matrix European ancestry-specific dosages

title: Split genotype matrix by ancestry — split_by_ancestry

what: Split unphased genotype matrix by ancestry for K populations — split_by_ancestry_multi
title: Split unphased genotype matrix by ancestry for K populations — split_by_ancestry_multi
text: Generalises split_by_ancestry arbitrary number ancestries.  variant, population allele proportions \(p_1, \ldots, p_K\) (summing 1) estimated samples unambiguous ancestry (pure-ancestry homozygotes heterozygotes, plus mixed hom-alts contribute one allele parent pool). Ambiguous heterozygotes mixed pair split conditional pairwise ratio \(p_i / (p_i + p_j)\). unambiguous alt alleles exist pair (singleton edge case), split defaults 0.5 / 0.5.

title: Split unphased genotype matrix by ancestry for K populations — split_by_ancestry_multi
code: split_by_ancestry_multi(gt_genotype, ancestry, pure_codes, mixed_codes)

title: Split unphased genotype matrix by ancestry for K populations — split_by_ancestry_multi
text: gt_genotype Integer matrix (variants x samples) genotypes (0/1/2). ancestry Integer matrix (variants x samples) ancestry codes, dimensions gt_genotype. pure_codes Named integer vector mapping population label pure-ancestry diploid code. Example: c(AFR = 3L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 4L). mixed_codes Data frame three columns: code Integer ancestry code mixed pair. pop1 Character name first parent population     (must name pure_codes). pop2 Character name second parent population     (must name pure_codes). One row per mixed-ancestry diploid type. Example 3 populations: data.frame(code=c(2L,5L,6L), pop1=c("AFR","AFR","EUR"), pop2=c("EUR","NAT","NAT"))

title: Split unphased genotype matrix by ancestry for K populations — split_by_ancestry_multi
text: Named list K numeric matrices (variants x samples), one per   population pure_codes, order.  element   named corresponding entry pure_codes.

title: Split unphased genotype matrix by ancestry for K populations — split_by_ancestry_multi
code: # 3-population example: AFR=3, EUR=1, NAT=4; mixed codes 2/5/6 set.seed(1) gt  <- matrix(sample(0:2, 20, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.8,.15,.05)),               nrow = 4, ncol = 5) # ancestry codes: 3=AFR/AFR, 1=EUR/EUR, 4=NAT/NAT, #                 2=AFR/EUR, 5=AFR/NAT, 6=EUR/NAT anc <- matrix(sample(c(1L,2L,3L,4L,5L,6L), 20, replace = TRUE),               nrow = 4, ncol = 5) pure  <- c(AFR = 3L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 4L) mixed <- data.frame(code = c(2L, 5L, 6L),                     pop1 = c("AFR", "AFR", "EUR"),                     pop2 = c("EUR", "NAT", "NAT")) out <- split_by_ancestry_multi(gt, anc, pure, mixed) names(out)   # "AFR" "EUR" "NAT" #> [1] "AFR" "EUR" "NAT"

what: Split phased haplotypes by ancestry — split_phased
title: Split phased haplotypes by ancestry — split_phased
text: Split phased haplotypes deterministically African European ancestry-specific dosage matrices.  haplotype's allele added whichever ancestry pool matches local ancestry call; haplotypes unrecognised ancestry codes contribute nothing either pool.

title: Split phased haplotypes by ancestry — split_phased
code: split_phased(   gt_hap0,   gt_hap1,   anc_hap0,   anc_hap1,   pop_codes = c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L) )

title: Split phased haplotypes by ancestry — split_phased
text: gt_hap0 Integer matrix (variants × samples) haplotype-0 alleles (0/1). gt_hap1 Integer matrix (variants × samples) haplotype-1 alleles (0/1). anc_hap0 Integer matrix (variants × samples) haplotype-0 ancestry codes. anc_hap1 Integer matrix (variants × samples) haplotype-1 ancestry codes. pop_codes Named integer vector length 2 giving AFR EUR ancestry codes used MSP file.  Defaults c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L) (RFMix convention).

title: Split phased haplotypes by ancestry — split_phased
text: List two numeric matrices: african African ancestry-specific dosage (variants × samples) european European ancestry-specific dosage (variants × samples)

title: Split phased haplotypes by ancestry — split_phased

what: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
title: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
text: Generalises split_phased arbitrary number ancestries. haplotype's allele routed dosage pool whose code matches local ancestry call.  Haplotypes unrecognised codes contribute 0 pools.  NA genotypes treated 0 (reference allele).

title: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
code: split_phased_multi(   gt_hap0,   gt_hap1,   anc_hap0,   anc_hap1,   pop_codes = c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L) )

title: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
text: gt_hap0 Integer matrix (variants x samples) haplotype-0 alleles (0/1). gt_hap1 Integer matrix (variants x samples) haplotype-1 alleles (0/1). anc_hap0 Integer matrix (variants x samples) haplotype-0 ancestry codes. anc_hap1 Integer matrix (variants x samples) haplotype-1 ancestry codes. pop_codes Named integer vector mapping population label ancestry code, e.g. c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 2L).  Codes must match used MSP file (RFMix default: 0-based integers).

title: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
text: Named list K numeric matrices (variants x samples), one per   entry pop_codes, order.  List names equal   names pop_codes.

title: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
text: two-population case split_phased_multi split_phased equivalent; prefer split_phased_multi new code may later extended three ancestries.

title: Split phased haplotypes into K population-specific dosage matrices — split_phased_multi
code: # Three-population panel: AFR=0, EUR=1, NAT=2 gt0 <- matrix(c(1L, 0L, 0L, 1L, 1L, 0L), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) gt1 <- matrix(c(0L, 1L, 1L, 0L, 0L, 1L), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) a0  <- matrix(c(0L, 1L, 2L, 0L, 1L, 2L), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) a1  <- matrix(c(1L, 2L, 0L, 2L, 0L, 1L), nrow = 3, ncol = 2) out <- split_phased_multi(gt0, gt1, a0, a1, c(AFR = 0L, EUR = 1L, NAT = 2L)) names(out)   # "AFR" "EUR" "NAT" #> [1] "AFR" "EUR" "NAT" out$NAT      # native-ancestry dosage matrix #>      [,1] [,2] #> [1,]    0    0 #> [2,]    1    0 #> [3,]    0    0
